title: Daily Summary — 2026-05-05
body:
## Recent Activity

Synced window: 2026-05-04T11:54:41Z to 2026-05-05T11:54:41Z. Signals show 13 issues, 12 PRs, 6 discussions, and 0 commits updated.

Activity centered on project-management/setup orchestration, Sepo version metadata, default automation/project-label settings, and a pivot from automatic failure reporting toward approval-gated failure diagnosis.

## Recently Active PRs

- [#157](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/157) merged: defaulted packaged automation mode to `agent` and project label application to enabled.
- [#158](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/158) open: adds approval-gated agent failure diagnosis; latest review shows minor remaining classifier-ordering concern.
- [#117](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/117) open: older automatic failure-reporting approach; discussion now points toward the safer diagnosis/approval flow in #158.
- [#139](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/139) merged: fixed trust for app-authored sub-orchestrator child markers.
- [#141](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/141), [#143](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/143), [#145](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/145), [#147](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/147), and [#149](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/149) remain open from the #126 setup/project-management workstream.
- [#133](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/133), [#134](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/134), and [#136](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/136) also remained active in the window.

## Recently Active Issues

- [#126](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/126) open: orchestration completed after creating staged child work for project planning/setup.
- [#155](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/155) closed: requested defaults were implemented via merged PR #157.
- [#156](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/156) open: requested a fresh approval-gated failure diagnosis implementation, now represented by PR #158.
- [#138](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/138) closed: sub-orchestrator parent resume bug was fixed via PR #139.
- Open child issues under #126 remained active: [#140](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/140), [#142](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/142), [#144](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/144), [#146](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/146), and [#148](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/148).
- [#26](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/26), [#127](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/127), [#132](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/132), and [#135](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/135) were also present in the synced issue signals.

## Recently Active Discussions

- [#154](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/154): discussed GitHub App install leading directly to Sepo actions; conclusion favored a hybrid hosted quick-start plus repo-local Actions path.
- [#150](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/150): proposed making public agents more interactive with broader `/answer` access.
- [#151](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/151): asked about installing Sepo into other repos directly from this repo.
- [#152](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/152): asked how to track libraries/repos installing or using Sepo.
- [#153](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/153): proposed a “yolo mode” where agents can approve PRs when appropriate.
- [#137](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/137): prior daily summary received follow-up activity.

## Follow-ups

- Drive PR #158 through the remaining review/fix loop, since it appears to supersede the older #117 failure-reporting behavior.
- Triage the open #126 child PRs for merge order so setup/project-management changes land cleanly.
- Convert discussion #154’s hybrid hosted/repo-local direction into a concrete follow-up issue if it is intended to guide product work.

---
*Generated by the agent-daily-summary workflow*

nodes.body:
## Project Management Summary

- Mode: `labels applied`
- Open items assessed: `39 issues, 13 pull requests`
- Managed labels: `priority/*`, `effort/*`

### Top Triage Queue

- `pull_request#158` Add approval-gated agent failure diagnosis — `priority/p1`, `effort/high`  
  High-impact failure-reporting workflow with active review/fix churn and direct closure path for #156. Label changes: none.

- `issue#156` Rethink bug resolution, automatic diagnosis, and automatic reporting — `priority/p1`, `effort/high`  
  Active product-safety and operational reliability work; now represented by PR #158. Label changes: none.

- `pull_request#149` Add deterministic setup apply route — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium`  
  Privileged setup mutation path in the active #126 stack; important but bounded. Label changes: none.

- `pull_request#147` Add plan-only setup route — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium`  
  Foundation for setup planning and later apply flow in the #126 project-management stack. Label changes: none.

- `pull_request#143` Add Project target config for project management — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium`  
  Core config boundary for Project-backed planning; blocks the intended setup/project-management direction. Label changes: none.

- `pull_request#141` Define minimal GitHub Project planning model — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium`  
  Establishes the planning model under parent #126 and supports later project-backed work. Label changes: none.

- `issue#126` What's the best way to manage the tasks and project planning for this repo now? — `priority/p1`, `effort/high`  
  Broad parent orchestration issue with many staged children and stacked PRs. Label changes: none.

- `pull_request#120` Add GitHub App installation bootstrap — `priority/p1`, `effort/high`  
  User-facing setup/distribution work with broad workflow impact. Label changes: none.

- `pull_request#116` Add scheduled Sepo update checks — `priority/p1`, `effort/high`  
  Important maintenance/update automation, non-draft and likely closer to merge actionability. Label changes: none.

- `issue#75` Agent review-body commands can be cancelled by sibling inline review-comment events — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium`  
  Concrete workflow reliability bug affecting review command execution. Label changes: none.

### Label Changes

No managed label changes were needed. Existing managed priority/effort labels are consistent with the current open issue and PR queue.

```json

  "label_changes": []
}
```

### Notes

Assessed all open issues and pull requests returned by GitHub. Several open child issues under #126 appear implemented by draft stacked PRs but remain open for normal PR closure flow, so their existing labels were preserved. PR #117 remains low priority because it is superseded by the newer #156/#158 approval-gated diagnosis direction.

### Managed Label Application

- Applied 0 managed priority/effort label operation(s).
